files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 4985f46..57a284e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,65 +1,51 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 3 Situation (Day 21)
-- Cash: $768,711
-- Subscribers: 89 (86 S1, 1 S2, 2 S3)
-- Plans: A=$3, B=$12, C=$25 (raised from $2/$5/$9 on day 14)
+## Week 4 Results (Day 28)
+- Cash: $757,135 (-$11,576 from week 3)
+- Subscribers: 170 (up from 89) - 81 new this week
+- Plans: A=$4, B=$19, C=$45 (raised on day 28)
 - Model tiers: A=3, B=4, C=5
-- Daily spend: Ops=$200, Dev=$800
-- Ad spend: S1=$300/day (social+search+content), S3=$100/day (social+search)
-- Weekly burn: ~$11,671
-- Conversion rate: 46/180 = 25.6% (down from 40% due to price increase)
-- Revenue this week: $248; Total monthly revenue: ~$1,080
-- Zero churn, zero downgrades
-
-## Key Insights
-- S1 is our ONLY converting segment: 44/46 new subs are S1
-- S3 ad spend is INEFFICIENT: only 2/38 S3 leads converted (5.3%) vs 31% for S1
-- S3 WTP is $193 but quality floor is 0.145 - our quality 0.248 exceeds it, but conversion is terrible
-- S2 remains locked (quality floor 0.37, our max 0.248)
-- 95% of new subscribers chose Plans A or B; only 6% chose Plan C
-- Average revenue per new subscriber: ~$6.20/month
-- At this rate, need 4,870+ subscribers to breakeven - unsustainable
-
-## Financial Reality Check
-- Weekly burn: ~$11,700 (dev $5,600 + ops $1,400 + ads $2,800 + capacity $1,505 + compute $524 + lead cost $90)
-- Weekly revenue: ~$250
-- Weekly net loss: ~$11,450
-- Days to breakeven at current trajectory: never (quality would need to unlock S2)
-
-## Strategy Pivots Needed
-1. STOP S3 ad spend immediately - $700-1050/week for 2 subscribers is terrible ROI
-2. REDIRECT all S3 budget to S1 for higher volume
-3. RAISE prices further - test if conversion stays acceptable
-4. Consider lowering Plan A slightly to boost volume while raising B/C
-5. Market research ($25K, 30%) to discover new segments
-6. R&D tier 1 completes ~day 30 - will give +0.029 quality boost
-
-## Cash Trajectory
-- Cash runway at current burn: ~67 weeks ($769K / $11.5K)
-- But need to unlock higher-value segments before cash depletes
-- R&D tier 1 cost: $166K (but already started, so sunk cost)
-- Next R&D tiers cost $333K+ (too expensive at current stage)
-
-## Week 3 Actions Summary
-- Raised prices from $2/$5/$9 to $3/$12/$25 on day 14
-- Maintained $800/day dev spend
-- Ran ad campaigns for S1 and S3
-- Social media post made
-- Market research NOT done (to conserve cash)
-
-## Risks & Opportunities
-- RISK: Price increase may have pushed S3 leads away despite quality being adequate
-- RISK: Burning cash faster than subscriber growth
-- RISK: Competitor events could raise S2 quality floor further
-- OPPORTUNITY: S1 market cap is 272K - huge growth runway
-- OPPORTUNITY: S3 WTP is very high ($193) - if we can improve conversion, huge upside
-
-## Week 4 Action Plan
-1. Reduce S3 ad spend to $0, increase S1 ad spend to $400/day
-2. Test raising Plan B to $15 and Plan C to $35 (keep A at $3 for acquisition)
-3. Consider a small price test on Plan A ($4 vs $3)
-4. Maintain dev spend at $800/day for steady quality improvement
-5. Make social media post about improvements
-6. Optionally do market research ($25K) for new segments (improve discovery rate)
-7. Monitor conversion rate closely - if drops below 20%, lower prices
+- S1 only converting: 81/234 leads = 35%
+- S2 locked (0 of 60 leads ever converted), S3 nearly locked (2 of 88)
+- Monthly revenue: ~$1,029 (ARPU $6.05)
+- R&D tier 1: in progress, expected completion ~day 30-32
+- Zero churn, zero downgrades, 24 open issues
+
+## Financial Reality
+- Weekly burn: ~$11,600
+- Ad spend: $400/day S1 only
+- Dev: $800/day, Ops: $200/day
+- CAC per new sub: ~$36 (6-month payback)
+- Cash runway: ~65 weeks at current burn
+
+## Key Strategic Decisions
+- S1-only ad strategy is working: $400/day = consistent 30-50 leads/day at 35% conversion
+- S3 ad spend ELIMINATED: 0 conversions from 86 leads = terrible ROI
+- Price increase test: $3/$15/$35 raised to $4/$19/$45 on day 28
+- If conversion drops below 25% next week, revert prices
+
+## Critical Dependency
+- R&D tier 1 completion: will add +~0.04 quality, pushing us to ~0.27
+- S2 quality floor = 0.37 - still locked even after R&D
+- Need dev spend ($800/day) + R&D to eventually unlock S2
+- S2 has WTP=60+ and higher plans = huge revenue potential
+
+## Forecast Strategy
+- 1 wk: Cash ~$745K (consistent $11.5K/week burn)
+- 4 wk: Cash ~$710K (if no R&D completion boost)
+- 12 wk: Cash ~$640K or better if S2 unlocks
+- 26 wk: Cash highly uncertain - depends on S2 unlock and churn
+
+## Lessons Learned
+1. S1 is our only viable segment right now - focus entirely on S1
+2. R&D is critical path to unlock higher-value segments
+3. Price increases seem to have minimal impact on S1 conversion
+4. Volume is more important than ARPU until S2/S3 unlock
+5. Social media posts may help with reputation but quantifiable impact unclear
+
+## Next Week Actions
+- Monitor conversion at new $4 price point
+- Watch for R&D tier 1 completion (should be imminent)
+- Maintain $400/day S1 ads, $800 dev, $200 ops
+- Consider capacity upgrade if usage approaches 200K (currently 142/sub/day = 24K total, well under 200K)
+- If R&D completes and quality jump is significant, consider testing S2 ads

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Check past few days of ad spend and leads
for day in range(22, 29):
    ads = nm.query(f'SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day = {day} AND category = "advertising"')
    if ads['rows'] and ads['rows'][0]['total'] is not None:
        print(f'Day {day}: ads=${-ads["rows"][0]["total"]:.0f}')
    else:
        print(f'Day {day}: ads=$0')
    
    leads = nm.query(f'SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE created_day = {day}')
    print(f'  Leads:', leads['rows'][0]['n'])

# Verify targeted ad spend is set properly 
print('\n=== Current targeted ad spend ===')
# We can only infer from daily ad totals and config_history
config = nm.query('SELECT day, ad_spend_social_media, ad_spend_search_ads, ad_spend_content_marketing FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1')
for row in config['rows']:
    print(row)

# Check what prices new subs will see
print('\nNew prices: A=$4, B=$19, C=$45')
